message:
perf(channel): replace FaultSender stats Mutex with per-counter AtomicU64

FaultSender previously guarded all four stats counters (messages_sent,
messages_reordered, messages_duplicated, reorder_flushes) behind a single
Mutex<FaultChannelStats>. Every send operation acquired this lock to bump
counters, creating unnecessary contention with concurrent stats() readers
and with the reorder buffer flush path (which also needs the stats lock).

This commit replaces the single Mutex with four independent AtomicU64
counters using Relaxed ordering:
- stat_messages_sent
- stat_messages_reordered
- stat_messages_duplicated
- stat_reorder_flushes

The record_sent(), record_reorder(), and record_duplication() helpers
now use fetch_add(1, Relaxed) instead of lock-then-increment. The
stats() reader constructs a FaultChannelStats snapshot from individual
atomic loads (the snapshot is not globally consistent across all four
counters, but this is acceptable for diagnostic counters — the previous
Mutex version also had TOCTOU between separate record_*() calls).

The Debug impl now calls self.stats() to format the snapshot rather
than printing the raw Mutex.

Additional: reorder_buffer pre-allocated with config.reorder_buffer_size
capacity to avoid reallocations during early sends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
